task_statement:
BRICK 6-surface audit repair, engine fix (task #8, write_scope-default half only -- confirmed by Smith plus a 4-agent adversarial re-verification workflow with a LIVE reproduction, see brick-6-graph-route-missing-default-write-scope-finding-0701.md).

THE DEFECT: brick build --graph (cli.py -> driver.py -> composition_compose.py::compose_building() -> run.py -> walker_kernel.py -- confirmed the ONLY official customer/operator route, not a side path) carries a graph node's write_scope VERBATIM if present, but applies NO DEFAULT and raises NO ERROR when a write-needed Brick node (requires_brick_write_scope: yes / capability_class != read) omits write_scope entirely. Effective write then silently resolves to false (support/connection/adapter_grant_policy.py derives write_effective = bool(request.write_scope)) with zero error and zero HOLD -- the Building reports frontier_kind=complete as if nothing were wrong, while the node silently could not write anything. Live-reproduced: a real brick build --graph run with a write-needed node lacking write_scope completed frontier_complete with zero files written, verified directly on disk.

A DIFFERENT code path, support/operator/assembly.py::assemble() (used ONLY by the onboard.py wizard flow, NEVER reachable from brick build --graph -- confirmed by exhaustive repo-wide callsite search), already does this correctly: when write_scope is None for a write-needed node, it calls _validated_write_scope() (assembly.py ~line 1326) which falls back to brick/spec.py::derived_worktree_write_scope() (~line 150), returning {"allowed_paths": ["."], "forbidden_paths": [".git/**"]}. This default applies UNIFORMLY regardless of Brick role (work vs QA/probe_write) -- it is NOT role-dependent, and it does not require per-task path precision to be safe. A graph author who wants a NARROWER, task-specific scope may still supply an explicit write_scope on the node, which overrides the default; the default only fills the gap when the node is silent about it.

THE FIX (this Building's sole scope): port the same default-on-omission behavior into the graph_packet materialization path -- most likely in support/operator/composition_compose.py::compose_building(), where write_scope is currently only transported verbatim (~line 982-984: raw_write_scope = brick.get("write_scope"); if raw_write_scope is not None: row["write_scope"] = ... -- needs an else-branch applying the same derived_worktree_write_scope() default whenever the Brick template declares requires_brick_write_scope truthy and the node supplied none), or wherever the design lanes determine is the single most correct seam. Reuse brick/spec.py::derived_worktree_write_scope() directly -- do not reimplement or duplicate the default logic in a second place.

OUT OF SCOPE for this Building (tracked separately as a follow-on): wiring the graph_topology_fan_barrier checker (already landed, see brick-6-graph-topology-fan-barrier-checker-closure-0701.md) into actual brick build --graph admission-time rejection. That is a related but distinct fix (checker invocation wiring, not write_scope value resolution) and must not be mixed into this Building's scope.

Checker-first: add a negative probe reproducing the exact live-reproduction scenario already recorded in the finding doc (a write-needed Brick node with no write_scope, run through brick build --graph, currently silently proceeds with write_effective=false and zero files written) -- must RED before the fix (prove the gap exists), GREEN after (prove the default now applies and the node CAN write within the default scope). Also add a probe proving an explicit narrower write_scope on a node still overrides the default (regression guard against always forcing the broad default onto every node).
selection_rule: Low-risk, purely additive checker task (goal doc tiering: low-risk/doc/cleanup phases may use a simpler shape than the mandatory dual-design fan-out reserved for named high-risk phases). Single design, single work, three-way final QA fan-out, closure.

nodes.work_statement: Design the write_scope default-on-omission fix per the task statement. Read support/operator/assembly.py's _validated_write_scope/derived_worktree_write_scope pattern, support/operator/composition_compose.py::compose_building(), and brick-6-graph-route-missing-default-write-scope-finding-0701.md in full. Propose the exact seam (composition_compose.py vs elsewhere), confirm it is reachable from brick build --graph (not a second non-canonical path), and confirm reuse (not duplication) of derived_worktree_write_scope(). No source edits.

Implement the write_scope default-on-omission fix in composition_compose.py::compose_building() (or the seam confirmed by design): when a Brick template declares requires_brick_write_scope truthy and the graph_packet node supplies no write_scope, apply brick/spec.py::derived_worktree_write_scope() as the default -- uniformly, not role-dependent. An explicit node-level write_scope must still override the default. Add the checker-first negative probe (RED before fix, GREEN after) plus the override-still-wins regression probe. Run py_compile and check_profile.py --all. Return changed_files and exact evidence.

Attack the checker implementation: confirm the negative fixture genuinely RED-fires with the new checker (not vacuously), confirm the positive fixture (P7d2/P7d3 barrier shape) is still accepted, confirm py_compile and check_profile.py --all pass. Probe_write only, no source mutation.

Confirm this checker stays support/checker mechanics only -- it may REJECT a bad graph_packet, but must not choose Movement, targets, success, or quality; it must not become a second engine or a second admission path duplicating plan_graph.py. Probe_write only.

Confirm evidence integrity: negative/positive fixtures are real and byte-distinct from prior QA bodies, proof_limits/not_proven present, no overclaiming of coverage beyond the fan-in/fan-out-barrier shape checked. Probe_write only.

Fan in code-qa, axis-qa, evidence-qa. Report observed_evidence / narrowly_proven / not_proven / next Movement candidate. Close only if the negative fixture RED-fires and the positive fixture stays accepted with current evidence; otherwise return Link-facing transition_concern_evidence and HOLD rather than smoothing it.

proof_limits: official brick build graph evidence is support evidence only
this Building closes only the write_scope-default half of task #8, not the barrier-checker admission-wiring half
not source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, or Movement authority
not_proven: whether the whole-worktree-minus-.git default is the ideal default for every future Brick kind, only that it is not worse than the current silent zero-write
P8 ship safety, P9 dynamic customer replay, fresh-machine install, real-provider reliability, customer comprehension
